assumptions: The analysis is for consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has already been declared and has created a recognised intragroup receivable/payable that is a monetary item.
The receivable is not assumed to be part of a net investment in a foreign operation.
No facts are provided on hedge documentation, instrument eligibility or effectiveness testing, so the analysis focuses on hedged-item eligibility.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 specifically allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend balance is a monetary receivable/payable.
The balance creates FX gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The entities involved have different functional currencies or otherwise retain residual consolidated FX exposure.
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met.
approaches.practical_implication: A hedge may be documented in consolidation only if the dividend receivable produces residual consolidated FX exposure.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 permits FX risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements; paragraphs 6.3.2 and 6.4.1 add measurability, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: General prohibition on intragroup hedged items in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: The default IFRS 9 rule is that, for hedge accounting, only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated in consolidated financial statements.
approaches.practical_implication: If the dividend receivable does not meet the specific intragroup monetary-item exception, no consolidated hedge designation is available.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 generally excludes intragroup items from being hedged in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction cash flow hedge
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 separately permits some highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to be hedged when their FX risk will affect consolidated profit or loss, but a booked dividend receivable is no longer a forecast transaction.
approaches.practical_implication: The relevant analysis is the monetary-item exception, not the forecast-transaction exception.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 and paragraphs B6.3.5–B6.3.6 provide a separate hedge model for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions affecting consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.reasoning: IFRIC 16 addresses hedges of net investments in foreign operations, where the hedged item is an amount of net assets of the foreign operation, not an ordinary intragroup dividend receivable.
approaches.practical_implication: Do not document this exposure under the net investment hedge model unless the designated hedged item is the net assets of a foreign operation.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 10–14 define a separate hedge model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations, not routine intragroup receivables.
